observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to intensify their efforts to reconcile their records and effect appropriate adjustments in their respective records/cashbook, when necessary, to establish the correct balance of Cash-Local Treasury Account and other affected accounts; and
Effect the necessary adjusting journal entries by debiting Due from Officers and Employees and crediting Cash-Local Treasury account amounting to the cash shortage balance.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to reconcile the accounts of the noted deficiencies and prepare an appropriate entry to record the adjustments. If a particular account had no more specific purpose, return immediately to the source funding agency. Henceforth, always ensure that financial transactions are well accounted for to assure reliability on the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Local Health Board to submit the list of frontline HRHs and public HWs, including BHWs, in relation to AHDP and SRA grant;
HRMO to submit the authority of HRHs and public HWs to physically report in the workstation on the period duration of the subject benefits, including the unsubmitted individual DTRs; and
MHO to submit copies of medical journals to support the claims of SRA. Otherwise, the total amount of benefits will be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer to submit an explanation detailing the events/circumstances wherein these so-called "other personnel" were indeed have been in direct contact with COVID-19 patients in those number of days they claimed in the payroll from September 2020 to June 2021. Otherwise, the said benefit provided to them will be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 and immediately submit the cash advance vouchers pertaining to Confidential Fund for CY 2021 to avoid possible suspension in the audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to religiously enforce and adhere to the prescribed procedures as stipulated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 to properly conduct inventory taking of its PPE accounts. Thereby, the prepared reports thereon would be trustworthy enough to substantiate the said accounts in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management:
Properly addressed the issues and concerns that hindered the implementation of these projects so that PPAs can all be implemented and carried out as planned; and
Review the balance of the continuing appropriation for this fund to determine savings from completed projects. Further, evaluate and re-assess the PPAs if still responsive to the priority needs of the constituents of the Municipality, otherwise reprogram the fund consistent with Paragraph 2, Section 322 of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant not to charge and pay similar-like expenditures using Municipal funds but instead hold the negligent personnel liable thereto. Likewise, instruct the MDRR Officer to religiously adhere to applicable laws, rules, and regulations by acquiring the required permits and licenses in operating communication radio equipment to avoid incurring penalties.

We recommended that Management direct all personnel concerned to:
Demand from the supplier the delivery of the subject items;
Impose liquidated damages to the supplier based on the undelivered items; and
Exercise prudence in every inspection of all forthcoming procurements.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials and employees to submit justification pertaining to the audit observations, otherwise the total amount of ₱590,232.67 be disallowed in the audit.

We recommended that Management instruct all the concerned personnel to strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take-home pay for government employees to boost the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct all concerned personnel to properly review the applications for monetization prior to processing the same. Hence, ensure strict compliance with the laws, rules, and regulations on the monetization of leave credits, specifically Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave, Rule XVI Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of EO No. 29.

Require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the following proposed adjusting journal entry:


Consistently review the correctness of the computation of depreciation and appropriately maintain Infrastructure Asset accounts in adherence to the mentioned guidelines. Henceforth, strict observance of the regulations in the proper keeping of accounts is enjoined.

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Assessor to assist the Municipal Engineer in the assessment of the fair value of the road lot component of local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the guidelines mentioned above about Hazard Pay incentives’ granting were not adhered to. Moreover, always ensure that applicable guidelines appropriate in dealing the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to effect applicable adjustments on the charging of the hazard pay incentives. Hence, exercise due diligence in reviewing obligations and ensure that expenditures were appropriately charged to the expense class applicable thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the appropriate office/s within the MLGU Barobo to consolidate and approve the Alternative Work Arrangements (AWAs), as prescribed by CSC Memorandum Circular No. 10, series of 2020, thereafter, furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of AWAs for reference in the audit.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the above-mentioned guidelines relevant in granting Special Risks Allowance were not adhered to. Henceforth, always ensure that applicable policies appropriate in dealing the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

Direct the Budget Officer to ensure that all subsequent financial assistance received are only utilized within its intended purpose.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review, prior to approval, disbursement processed against applicable laws, rules, and regulations to avoid possible disallowance in audit.

Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and

Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor's Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended Management to purchase drugs and medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary Volume I.  If the needed drugs were not on the list, write a letter to the Head of the National Drug Policy Office the request to purchase. Henceforth, strict compliance of Administrative Order (AO) No. 2015-0051, Executive Order (EO) No. 49, and Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require all offices concerned not to specify brand names in its Purchase Request but instead state specifications based on relevant characteristics, functionality, and performance requirements.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to (1) require the contractors to submit Certification from the Insurance Commission (2) post, through its Secretariat necessary notices and contracts in the PhilGEPS (3) send letters invitation to the three required observers in all stages of procurements (4) conduct pre-procurement conference for negotiations that it is required to do so and; (5) ensure the submission of complete documentary requirements supporting the perfected contracts.

Require the contractors of 5 infrastructure project to post warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failures; and

Henceforth, strictly observe and enforce the provisions required by RA 9184 and its IRR specifically its requirements on the posting of warranty securities.

Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on timely submission of copies of Contracts/Purchase Orders and supporting documents to the COA Office; and

Notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance so that timely inspection can be undertaken.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transfer of funds to barangays be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of funds.

Discontinue the payment of cash gift earlier than the prescribed period;

Update themselves on the existing guidelines on the payment of cash gift and other emoluments for Elected Officials and Employees, particularly DBM Circular No. 2016-4 and Local Budget Circular No. 121, to ensure proper release of the said benefits.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Accounting Staff to expedite the filing of the necessary supporting documents and ensure that the succeeding disbursement vouchers be submitted within the prescribed period.

Ensure the appropriate imposition of income tax to all granted benefits and allowances of the Municipality; and

withhold from the salaries of concerned employees, as listed in Annex “I”, the uncollected P546,662.15 income tax and remit thereafter to the BIR.

Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.

Municipal Accountant to post adjustments in the books of accounts that corresponds to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the calendar year.

Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to intensify their efforts to reconcile their records and effect appropriate adjustments in their respective records/cashbook, when necessary, to establish the correct balance of Cash-Local Treasury Account and other affected accounts.

Require the Municipal Accountant to review the entries affecting the negative balances of Cash-Local Treasury Account and ensure the fairness of the presentation of the said account.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to faithfully record all the transactions of Cash Local Treasury Accounts to the Cashbook-Cash in Treasury and reflect the correct balance of Cash on hand at a certain date.

Require the MEE Administrator, in consultation with the MEEDO Board, to implement strategies that will ensure that selling and sub-leasing of stall rights will be prevented. Moreover, formulate strategies that will ensure that the occupancy fee will be collected from the newly qualified occupants and maximize the income potentials of the stalls/booths within the Bus/Jeep Terminal.

Require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders, and if the delinquent stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the MEEDO Code of Barobo.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination with appropriate Office, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

Expedite the filing of the necessary supporting documents to be attached in the liquidation reports for 2017 and 2018 cash advances and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor.

Ensure that the succeeding liquidation reports be submitted within the prescribed period as prescribed under the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations. 

If necessary, make a representation with the proper authorities for the hiring of additional personnel to augment the existing manpower resources of the Municipal Accounting Office.

Instruct the HRMO- Designate to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations

Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.

Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,

Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.

Require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Barobo and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT).

Require the Municipal Accountant and Designated Property Officer, to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

Require the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books of accounts the identified understatement/overstatement by drawing the following proposed adjusting journal entry:
Consistently review the correctness of the computation of depreciation and appropriately maintain Infrastructure Asset accounts in adherence to the mentioned guidelines. Henceforth, strict observance of the regulations in the proper keeping of accounts is enjoined.

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide a complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
For existing road networks, direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Assessor in the determination of the fair value of road lot component for all local roads under the jurisdiction of the Municipal Government.
Direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize the amount pertaining to the road lot as part of the reported book value of the Road Networks account.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the guidelines mentioned above about Hazard Pay incentives' granting were not adhered to. Moreover, always ensure that applicable guidelines appropriate in dealing the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to effect applicable adjustments on the charging of the hazard pay incentives. Hence, exercise due diligence in reviewing obligations and ensure that expenditures were appropriately charged to the expense class applicable thereof.

We recommended that Management direct the appropriate office/s within the MLGU Barobo to consolidate and approve the Alternative Work Arrangements (AWAs), as prescribed by CSC Memorandum Circular No. 10, series of 2020, thereafter, furnish the Office of the Auditor copies of AWAs for reference in the audit.

We recommended that Management submit a written explanation justifying why the above-mentioned guidelines relevant in granting Special Risks Allowance were not adhered to. Henceforth, always ensure that applicable policies appropriate in dealing the affairs of the LGU shall be observed to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in the audit.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Budget Officer to ensure that all subsequent financial assistance received are only utilized within its intended purpose.
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to carefully review, prior to approval, disbursement processed against applicable laws, rules, and regulations to avoid possible disallowance in audit.

We recommended that Management strengthen its monitoring controls by requiring the personnel in-charge of donations to:
Ensure issuance of Acknowledgment Receipts to all future receipts of donations in-kind to confirm that donations are properly recorded and accounted for; 
Ensure that all future issuances of donated items are properly supported with Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any document/record to prove receipt by and distribution to the intended beneficiaries; and
Determine the inventory of remaining undistributed items, if any, for recording in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.

We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the Social Welfare Officer to
Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and
Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor's Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.

We recommended Management to purchase drugs and medicines that are within the Philippine National Drug Formulary Volume I.  If the needed drugs were not on the list, write a letter to the Head of the National Drug Policy Office the request to purchase. Henceforth, strict compliance of Administrative Order (AO) No. 2015-0051, Executive Order (EO) No. 49, and Philippine National Drug Formulary (PNDF) Volume.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require all offices concerned not to specify brand names in its Purchase Request but instead state specifications based on relevant characteristics, functionality, and performance requirements.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC to (1) require the contractors to submit Certification from the Insurance Commission (2) post, through its Secretariat necessary notices and contracts in the PhilGEPS (3) send letters invitation to the three required observers in all stages of procurements (4) conduct pre-procurement conference for negotiations that it is required to do so and; (5) ensure the submission of complete documentary requirements supporting the perfected contracts.

We recommended the Management to:
Require the contractors of 5 infrastructure project to post warranty security to guarantee their performance in the event of eventual structural defects/failures; and
Henceforth, strictly observe and enforce the provisions required by RA 9184 and its IRR specifically its requirements on the posting of warranty securities.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on timely submission of copies of Contracts/Purchase Orders and supporting documents to the COA Office; and
Notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance so that timely inspection can be undertaken.

We recommended Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all transfer of funds to barangays be covered by a Memorandum of Agreement and other documentary requirements as enumerated in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001, to establish proper accountability and monitoring of the proper utilization of funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Accounting Staff to expedite the filing of the necessary supporting documents and ensure that the succeeding disbursement vouchers be submitted within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure the appropriate imposition of income tax to all granted benefits and allowances of the Municipality; and
Withhold from the salaries of concerned employees, as listed in Annex "Q", the uncollected P546,662.15 income tax and remit thereafter to the BIR.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:

Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.

Municipal Accountant to post adjustments in the books of accounts that corresponds to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the calendar year.

Ensure that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the books using the appropriate expenses accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.

observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to prepare a schedule of unreleased/unclaimed checks at the end of the reporting period and submit thereafter to the Municipal Accountant.
Municipal Accountant to draw an adjustment for journal entries in the books of accounts that correspond to the unreleased or unclaimed checks at every end of the reporting period.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Prepare an adjusting journal entry to correct the misstatement of the affected accounts by debiting prior-period adjustment and crediting the appropriate expense accounts.
Review all journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year; and
Ensure that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the books using the appropriate expenses accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.

We recommended Management to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to intensify their efforts to reconcile their records and effect appropriate adjustments in their respective records/cashbook, when necessary, to establish the correct balance of Cash-Local Treasury Account and other affected accounts.
Require the Municipal Accountant to review the entries affecting the negative balances of Cash-Local Treasury Account and ensure the fairness of the presentation of the said account.
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to faithfully record all the transactions of Cash Local Treasury Accounts to the Cashbook-Cash in Treasury and reflect the correct balance of Cash on hand at a certain date.

We recommended that Management require the MEE Administrator to:
Ensure that the Notices of Vacancy of Market Stalls are posted in conspicuous places in compliance with Section 48 of the MEEDO Code; and
Coordinate with the MEEDO Board and formulate strategies that will make sure that all stalls are leased by qualified stallholders, to maximize the income potentials of the stalls/booths within the public market.

In the conference, it was explained by the Municipal Economic Enterprise (MEE) Administrator that the unoccupied stalls actually have owners, however those owners have discontinued doing business in the Public Market. He said further that they already communicated with the vacant stalls owners to inform them on the issue and assured that before the end of March 2020, a notice of vacancy shall be posted.

We recommended that Management require the MEE Administrator to:
Instruct the Market Supervisor to assess and collect daily rental fees based on the rates prescribed under MEEDO Code; and
Coordinate with the MEEDO Board to formulate plans in updating/amending the rate of rental fees to be collected from market stall rentals.

During the exit meeting, it was commented by the Management that the MEEDO Board, spearheaded by no less than the Municipal Mayor, has conducted two meetings after they received the COA audit finding. As a result, a revised rental rate was proposed and was submitted to the Sangguniang Bayan for approval.
The Municipal Vice-Mayor affirmed that they have received the proposal from the MEEDO Board. He assured everyone that Sangguniang Bayan will expedite the matter at hand.

We recommended that Management require the MEE Administrator, in consultation with the MEEDO Board, to implement strategies that will ensure that selling and sub-leasing of stall rights will be prevented. Moreover, formulate strategies that will ensure that the occupancy fee will be collected from the newly qualified occupants and maximize the income potentials of the stalls/booths within the Bus/Jeep Terminal.

The Management explained that the provision on occupancy fee for a stall in the Bus/Jeep Terminal was not included in the MEEDO Code. In their meetings with the MEEDO Board, the related occupancy fee was also proposed to be incorporated in the revised MEEDO code. He further explained that they have informed the stall owners that subleasing is not permitted and requiring them to submit the contract of lease.

We recommended Management to require the MEE Administrator to strictly impose the collection of the balances from the delinquent stallholders, and if the delinquent stallholders failed to pay after due notifications, revoke the contract of lease, in consonance with the MEEDO Code of Barobo.

In his reply to our audit findings from paragraphs 5 to 8, the MEE Administrator transpired that they have noted the deficiencies and lapses in implementing provisions from the MEEDO Code. This is due to various factors that are beyond their control. He further extended his strong commitment to comply with the audit recommendations and assured the Team that all deficiencies will be brought to the attention of the MEEDO Board and Sangguniang Bayan for appropriate consideration.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Mayor mentioned that the MEE Administrator has apprised the MEEDO Board on the audit findings. The MEEDO Board with coordination with the concerned stallholders, is now taking corrective actions to address the deficiencies noted. As a timeline to implement the recommendations, Management mentioned that the recommendations will be implemented on or before March 31, 2020.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer, with coordination with appropriate Office, to ensure that local tax rates are updated every five years pursuant to Section 191 of the  Local Government Code, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the concerned Offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.

The Local Chief Executive (LCE) informed the Audit Team, during the exit conference, that there is an ongoing move on revising the Local Tax Code. It is also confirmed by the Vice-Mayor and Sangguniang Bayan Members that before the end of March 2020, they will schedule a Public Hearing on the proposed revision.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Ensure appropriate application of tax exemption and impose income tax to voluntary Pag-IBIG contributions; and
Deduct and withhold from the salaries of concerned employees, as listed in Annex K, the uncollected ₱123,873.10 income tax and remit thereafter to the BIR.

The Management agreed with the audit recommendations and henceforth apply to their computation of withholding tax per employee in the succeeding years. However, they categorically asked the Team, if they can forego the previous years' tax deficiency or should they compute and remit the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR).
The Audit Team clarified that the issue can only be answered by the BIR Personnel. It was further suggested that Management make a representation to the BIR, in regard to the matter at hand.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and all other personnel concerned to strengthen the internal control system on fuel consumption and ensure that all disbursements are properly and completely supported with documents to establish the correctness of the payments.

The Management agreed with the recommendation of the Audit Team and averred that they have complied with recommendations by establishing a fuel request policy. They further inquired the Audit Team if the submitted fuel request policy is acceptable as per audit is concerned. They further asked the Team if there is any regulation that would provide a particular form that could suit the requirements of a sound internal control system and would also provide necessary documentary requirements for the matter.
The COA Team informed Management that the fuel request policy is still under the validation by the Team. The team will assess if the current policy can effectively control the fuel consumption of the Municipality. The Team added that there is a certain guideline that sets standards regarding the consumption of fuel. So, it was suggested that Management must set limitations and standards to be followed to fit its operational requirements.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply with the aforementioned rules and regulations in the hiring of Job Orders, and:
Issue policies and guidelines in hiring JO and Contract of Service of Personnel in Legislative and Executive Offices;
Ensure that the hiring of JOs personnel be put into an agreement by entering into Job Order and incorporated therein the services to be rendered and the number of man-hours, quantity, and duration of the work to be performed;
Strengthen the controls in the hiring of job order by requiring them to submit personal records, undergo an interview, and document the evaluation of performance to give assurance that the risk identified above are address; and
Require all JOs to submit a properly prepared accomplishment report based on their actual duties and activities performed and to use computer-generated DTRs based on the captured biometric data, except for those in the field, for the establishment of a reliable number of days worked as the basis for the computation of their wages.

The LCE commented in the exit meeting that they would like to settle this issue as soon as possible and would like to carry out what the audit recommendation suggested. He asked the Audit Team to provide guidelines in hiring JO so they can be guided. He also added that concerned offices such as the Human Resources Management Office (HRMO) and the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan should be communicated on matters relating to personnel information.
The Vice Mayor assured the Audit Team that they also conducted the same practice with the HRMO, however, filing of documents was only kept in their office. He further added that he will order his staff handling the personnel records and other Sangguniang Bayan Members to submit all records pertaining to their personnel to the HRMO.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

Expedite the filing of the necessary supporting documents to be attached in the liquidation reports for 2017 and 2018 cash advances and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor.

Ensure that the succeeding liquidation reports be submitted within the prescribed period as prescribed under the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations. 

If necessary, make a representation with the proper authorities for the hiring of additional personnel to augment the existing manpower resources of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommend that Management:

Instruct the MSWDO to update themselves in the latest guidelines related to the grant of Hazard Pay. In the succeeding preparation of payrolls for hazard pay, observe the latest guidelines as set by the proper authority.

Require the Municipal Accountant to examine the correctness and validity of the claims before approving the disbursement vouchers for payment.

We recommend that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 by:

Requiring in-depth study of the proposed programs and projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

Prioritizing projects which are doable, realizable and within the capability of the LGU to implement within the targeted completion period.

We recommend that Management ensure that the items/expenses to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are compliant with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01, in order that the envisioned socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes shall be achieved.

We recommend that Management:

Instruct the HRMO- Designate to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively. 

Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations

Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.

We recommend that Management require the MDRRM Officer to:

Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,

Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.

We recommend Management to require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Barobo and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT).

We recommend Management to:

Require the Municipal Accountant and Designated Property Officer, to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.

Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.

Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to record the completed infrastructure projects to the appropriate asset account under the General Fund books of the municipality to present fairly the PPE and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of Collection Negotiation Agreement (CNA) to its employees unless the submission of CNA which is duly approved by the Civil Service Commission. In addition, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer meticulously inspect the completeness of the legal documents before processing the payment of CNA to prevent wastage of government funds

We recommended that Management install and establish strict internal control on the remittance and deposit of collections. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the revenue collectors to use prescribed forms for reporting collections and remittances/deposit, remit/deposit their collections at the close of each business day or the next banking day and to regularly update their cashbooks. In addition, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to properly support the summary of collections and deposits with the RCDs received from the revenue collectors. Finally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously check the completeness of used accountable forms reported by the revenue collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prevent incomplete reporting of collections and remittances.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to affect the necessary correcting entry to recognize the cash advances granted under the Receivable accounts, and immediately submit the liquidation reports for the said cash advances for verification. In addition, strictly observe the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for its officials and employees.

We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 relative to the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure efficient output that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the locality and implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance of P19,593,121.27 as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and the Management to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize programs and projects under SWMP.

We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council require the designated LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary project implementation schedule and/or work and financial plan for each PPAs to efficiently monitor its implementation and to take corrective measures to fast-track its accomplishment. In addition, encourage the designated LDRRM officer to attend seminars/trainings related to DRRM to efficiently and effectively program the PPAs of the LDRRM that would result to disaster-resilient municipality.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

We recommended that Management install a system for hiring job order personnel based on the necessity and urgency of each department, and require the submission of complete documentation for filing and reference for payment of their wages.

We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

We recommended that Management stop the payment of Collection Negotiation Agreement (CNA) to its employees unless the submission of CNA which is duly approved by the Civil Service Commission. In addition, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer meticulously inspect the completeness of the legal documents before processing the payment of CNA to prevent wastage of government funds
observations.reason: No CNA duly approved by the CSC submitted to COA Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management install and establish strict internal control on the remittance and deposit of collections. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the revenue collectors to use prescribed forms for reporting collections and remittances/deposit, remit/deposit their collections at the close of each business day or the next banking day and to regularly update their cashbooks. In addition, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to properly support the summary of collections and deposits with the RCDs received from the revenue collectors. Finally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously check the completeness of used accountable forms reported by the revenue collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prevent incomplete reporting of collections and remittances.
observations.reason: Huge amount of cash in treasury account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 relative to the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure efficient output that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the locality and implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance of P19,593,121.27 as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in 2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.
observations.reason: No Business Plan submitted to COA Office

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council require the designated LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary project implementation schedule and/or work and financial plan for each PPAs to efficiently monitor its implementation and to take corrective measures to fast-track its accomplishment. In addition, encourage the designated LDRRM officer to attend seminars/trainings related to DRRM to efficiently and effectively program the PPAs of the LDRRM that would result to disaster-resilient municipality.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY2018 AAR

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management install a system for hiring job order personnel based on the necessity and urgency of each department, and require the submission of complete documentation for filing and reference for payment of their wages.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
expedite the filing of the necessary supporting documents to be attached in the liquidation reports for 2017 and 2018 cash advances and immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor.
ensure that the succeeding liquidation reports be submitted within the prescribed period as prescribed under the aforementioned laws, rules and regulations.
if necessary, make a representation with the proper authorities for the hiring of additional personnel to augment the existing manpower resources of the Municipal Accounting Office.

We recommend that Management:
Instruct the MSWDO to update themselves in the latest guidelines related to the grant of Hazard Pay. In the succeeding preparation of payrolls for hazard pay, observe the latest guidelines as set by the proper authority.
Require the Municipal Accountant to examine the correctness and validity of the claims before approving the disbursement vouchers for payment.

We recommend that Management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 by:
Require in-depth study of the proposed programs and projects before inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.
Prioritize projects which are doable, realizable and within the capability of the LGU to implement within the targeted completion period.

We recommend that Management ensure that the items/expenses to be funded by the 20% Development Fund are compliant with the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01, in order that the envisioned socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes shall be achieved.

We recommend that Management:
Instruct the HRMO- Designate to require the JO/COS to submit Tax Identification Number (TIN) and Annual Registration Fee (ARF) upon hiring and renewal of contracts, respectively.
Require the Municipal Accountant to compute, withhold and remit the respective percentage taxes of JO/COS Personnel for CY 2018, as provided in RMC 51-2018 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations
Capacitate the Municipal Accounting Personnel, by requiring them to attend seminars and trainings related to the latest updates on Tax and BIR Rulings.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRM Officer to:
Install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PAPs identified in the DRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation,
Conduct in-depth studies for previously identified project and determine if these are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign these funds to other disaster related PPAs which are implementable within the year.

We recommended Management to require the appropriate office to ensure that all Lands owned by the municipality are duly registered under the name LGU Barobo and secure copies of the corresponding Torrens Certificate of Title (TCT).

We recommend Management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant and Designated Property Officer, to fast-track the reconciliation of their records and thereafter, ensure that these balances are reconciled at year-end.
Ensure that the Property Officer, together with the Inventory Committee, conduct an actual physical count of PPEs, annually.
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that properties are properly recorded and capitalized, thereafter, apply appropriate systematic depreciation of assets.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to record the completed infrastructure projects to the appropriate asset account under the General Fund books of the municipality to present fairly the PPE and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Various completed infrastructure projects were not yet recorded to the appropriate asset account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to affect the necessary correcting entry to recognize the cash advances granted under the Receivable accounts, and immediately submit the liquidation reports for the said cash advances for verification. In addition, strictly observe the guidelines on the grant and liquidation of cash advances for its officials and employees.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the construction of potable water system under the Salintubig program, and conduct the necessary activities to make the potable water system projects effective and usable by the expected beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management strictly observe the procedures of implementation of projects funded from the Local Government Support Fund. In addition, instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the modification of the program of works of the projects being delayed to efficiently and effectively deliver the expected benefits to the prospective beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Projects funded from LGSF were implemented by contract

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly observe the implementation of programs, projects and activities for Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) including its appropriation, and instruct the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children as basis for the incorporation of PPAs to the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Attributions were made to support LCPC programs

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Local School Board that expenditures for sports activities in a form of cash advance be given to a Special Disbursing Officer who is duly bonded, and to submit in detail the program design of the activity together with the event of every athlete.
observations.reason: Cash advances were handled by duly bonded Special Disbursing Officer of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and the Management to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize programs and projects under SWMP.

We recommended that Management conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; enforce mandatory segregation of solid wastes at the source; and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection as mandated by RA 9003 or Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements on the collection and transport of solid waste and provide appropriation for the procurement of personal protective equipment for waste collectors including the provision of trainings related to proper solid waste management under the Ecological Solid Waste Management of the LGU. In addition, consider the provision of compartments to its two designated dump trucks for waste collection.

We recommended that Management maintain a separate account and Annual Operating Budget for each Local Economic Enterprises to determine its feasibility and take appropriate measures to improve its operation.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
observations.reason: The LGU had appointed regular plantilla position for MDRRMO only

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.
observations.reason: Although payroll sheets still showed net pay below P4,000, loan applications are already monitored upon renewal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice, and adjust the Municipality’s share at P100.00 per month per permanent employee as mandated by Republic Act No. 9679.

We recommended that Management:

Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the physical count of all of its PPE owned; prepare the RPCPPE and reconcile the same to the records maintained in the Office of the Municipal Accountant; 

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPELCs of PPE accounts including the Road Network account to determine the projects transferred from the Trust Fund to the General Fund books; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entry after the reconciliation of its records with the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory Committee of the Municipality failed to conduct periodic physical count of all the PPEs of the LGU.

observations.reason: As of December 31, 2017, the LGU still failed to efficiently utilized its 20% Development Fund.
The PPMP and APP were prepared not in accordance with the prescribed form by the GPPB.
We reiterated the observations on CY 2017 audit as a result of the conduct of cash examination during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Accountant should strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly and quarterly reports.
observations.reason: Concerned offices still failed to observe the prescribed timeline for the submission of approved and paid DVs and liquidated payrolls.

observations.recommendation: 1.6.	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to effect adjusting entries to record the completed infrastructure projects to the appropriate asset account under the General Fund books of the municipality to present fairly the PPE and Government Equity accounts in the financial statements.

2.7. 	We recommended that Management stop the payment of Collection Negotiation Agreement (CNA) to its employees unless the submission of CNA which is duly approved by the Civil Service Commission. In addition, the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Budget Officer meticulously inspect the completeness of the legal documents before processing the payment of CNA to prevent wastage of government funds.

3.7. 	We recommended that Management install and establish strict internal control on the remittance and deposit of collections. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the revenue collectors to use prescribed forms for reporting collections and remittances/deposit, remit/deposit their collections at the close of each business day or the next banking day and to regularly update their cashbooks. In addition, instruct the Municipal Treasurer to properly support the summary of collections and deposits with the RCDs received from the revenue collectors. Finally, instruct the Municipal Accountant to meticulously check the completeness of used accountable forms reported by the revenue collectors and the Municipal Treasurer to prevent incomplete reporting of collections and remittances.

5.6. 	We recommended that Management adhere strictly to the guidelines provided under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 relative to the appropriation and utilization of 20% Development Fund to ensure efficient output that would improve more the socio-economic condition of the locality and implement immediately the programs and projects pertinent to the unutilized balance of P19,593,121.27 as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan.

6.9. 	We recommended that Management strictly observe the guidelines on the construction of potable water system under the Salintubig program, and conduct the necessary activities to make the potable water system projects effective and usable by the expected beneficiaries.

7.4. 	We recommended that Management strictly observe the procedures of implementation of projects funded from the Local Government Support Fund. In addition, instruct the Municipal Engineer to fast track the modification of the program of works of the projects being delayed to efficiently and effectively deliver the expected benefits to the prospective beneficiaries.

8.6. 	We recommended that Management strictly observe the implementation of programs, projects and activities for Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) including its appropriation, and instruct the LCPC to prepare the Local Development Plan for Children as basis for the incorporation of PPAs to the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.

9.7. 	We recommended that Management instruct the Local School Board that expenditures for sports activities in a form of cash advance be given to a Special Disbursing Officer who is duly bonded, and to submit in detail the program design of the activity together with the event of every athlete.

10.7. 	We recommended that Management strictly comply with the minimum requirements for the operation of controlled dumps; include in its Ecological Solid Waste Management Program appropriation for the establishment of sanitary landfill; and the Management to prioritize the implementation of solid waste management program by providing enough budget in order to realize programs and projects under SWMP.

11.8. 	We recommended that Management conduct intensive information and education campaign on the proper segregation of solid wastes; enforce mandatory segregation of solid wastes at the source; and strictly comply with the minimum requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collection as mandated by RA 9003 or Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000.

13.6. 	We recommended that Management instruct the manager of the Local Economic Enterprise to prepare a 5-Year Business Plan for each local economic enterprise maintained and operated by the local government unit as basis for the operationalization of the annual operating budget of each LEEs to ensure that programs, activities and projects will contribute to its effective and efficient operation and the achievement of the LEEs goals and objectives.

14.6. 	We recommended that Management maintain a separate account and Annual Operating Budget for each Local Economic Enterprises to determine its feasibility and take appropriate measures to improve its operation.

15.6. 	We recommended that Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council require the designated LDRRM Officer to prepare the necessary project implementation schedule and/or work and financial plan for each PPAs to efficiently monitor its implementation and to take corrective measures to fast-track its accomplishment. In addition, encourage the designated LDRRM officer to attend seminars/trainings related to DRRM to efficiently and effectively program the PPAs of the LDRRM that would result to disaster-resilient municipality.

16.7. 	We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.

18.6. 	We recommended that the Management strictly observe the provisions of the law regarding the mandated monthly net take home pay for government employees to boast the morale and productivity of its employees in government service.

19.5. 	We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice, and adjust the Municipality’s share at P100.00 per month per permanent employee as mandated by Republic Act No. 9679.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant re-compute the depreciation and the accumulated depreciation of all items of the PPE, and to make an adjusting entry to correct the overstatement and/or understatement to fairly present the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant made an adjusting entry to some of PPE items with erroneous computation of depreciation.
observations.reason: Not all of the PPEs were computed the correct depreciation and adjusted by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
The Municipal Treasurer submit promptly the Detailed Estimates of Income and Expenditures on or before the fifteenth (15th) day of July of each year in accordance to Section 315(a) of the code; and

The Local School Board to observe the prescribed period on the preparation of its annual budget to prevent unusual delay on the implementation of its primary education programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: The LSB coordinated to the MT for the prompt submission of the report on the prescribed period.
observations.reason: The MT submitted the report of Detailed Income and Expenditures on the prescribed period. The LSB planned judiciously the PPAs to be included in the SEF which was implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop the practice of charging to the 20% Development Fund expenses which are not related to and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures. The municipal officials are enjoined to follow strictly the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DBM and DILG in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: There are still expenditures incurred which are ineligible to be charged against the Fund.

observations.managementAction: The barangay governments were required to submit POW for their projects to be funded by the LGU.
observations.reason: POWs and MOA between the LGU and BLGU were not forwarded for review and examination.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct the Office of the Municipal Engineer to fast-track the preparation and approval of the Program of Works for these programs/projects; and

Instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to prepare and finalize the time table for the implementation of these programs/projects.
observations.managementAction: The LGU implemented some PPAs funded by the LGSF
observations.reason: There are projects/ programs that were implemented and completed during the year. However, four projects was partially implemented and/or remained unimplemented as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant record the total unutilized LDRRM balance to the appropriate account to present fairly its financial statements, and to maintain a subsidiary ledger for each year of transfer pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant effected an adjusting entry to recognized the unutilized LDRRM fund to its appropriate account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMC incorporate the utilization of the previous years’ unutilized LDRRM fund in the preparation of the current year Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP), and to review and update annually its utilization to optimally use the said fund for disaster risk reduction and management activities pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMC prepared a resolution and approved by the local sanggunian on the re-programming of the unutilized LDRRM funds.
observations.reason: The LDRRMC failed to prepare the LDRRM Investment Plan for the re-programmed unused balance of LDRRM to be approved by the local sanggunian of the LGU.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality required the offices to prepare work and financial plan as bases for the procurement of petroleum products in accordance with the set timeline.
observations.reason: Not all offices prepared and submitted the Work and Financial Plan to support the procurement of petroleum products during the targeted timeline.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly observe the revised implementing rules and regulations of Republic Act No. 9184 without prejudice to the suspension of the transaction for non-submission of the required documents by the responsible persons.
observations.reason: Submission of the required documentation was not completely submitted.
There are Purchase Orders and its supporting documents which were not completely filled up with the necessary information on the face of the documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the focal point person to spearhead the establishment of a GAD Database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the Municipality. In addition, assist and/or support the GAD focal person to attend seminars regarding Gender and Development to effectively dispose of her function.
observations.managementAction: The LGU established its GAD Focal Point System and the GAD Plan was duly reviewed and endorsed by the DILG.
observations.reason: GAD Database was not yet prepared by the GAD Executive Committee and its TWG.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the LGU management cause the immediate submission of the contracts and its integral parts to facilitate the auditorial review and evaluation of the contract and any deficiencies can be rectified on time. Submit perfected contracts within the reglementary period as provided in COA Circular No. 2009-001.
observations.managementAction: No comments received.
observations.reason: Those contracts were not forwarded and/or returned for review. The approved contracts were not submitted within the prescribed period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management prioritize the conduct of physical inventory taking and reconciliation of Property, Plant and Equipment and immediately submit the RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: No comments received.
observations.reason: See observation # 16 for the full discussion.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

a. Re-visit the guidelines set forth under COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 particularly on the identification of programs/projects chargeable to Peace and Order Program, since this is the basis in the computation of the 30% allocation for confidential fund of the LGU.

b. Instruct the agency Accountant or the authorized representative to submit the cash advance disbursement voucher and the necessary documents within the prescribed period for post audit of the cash advance for confidential fund.
c. Observe the guidelines on the Grant and Liquidation of Cash Advance for CF.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured to comply with the regulations provided under the Joint Circular.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, the DVs for the cash advance of Confidential Fund were still submitted beyond the prescribed period. There are still items included in the POP which are not related to peace and order program of the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observe the guidelines in the utilization of the Special Education Fund (SEF) provided under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 and 01-A in the formulation of the Local School Board budget.
observations.managementAction: The LSB assured to prioritize expenditures chargeable against the SEF.
observations.reason: For CY 2016, there are still expenditures incurred which are deemed ineligible to be charged against the SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the submission of liquidation report of the funds transferred to the Athletic Association.  Henceforth, all fund transfers shall be recorded as receivables to monitor the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management demanded the Unit 1 District Athletic Association the liquidation of the fund transfer given.
observations.reason: The Accountant recognized the fund transfer as Other Receivables. Likewise, liquidation were made by the Athletic Association.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to hasten the submission of their accounts and financial reports and to follow strictly the regulations governing the submission of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The LCE directed the Municipal Accountant to promptly submit the accounts within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: See observation # 14 in Part II of this Report for the full discussion.

observations.recommendation:
a. The Municipal Health Officer strictly observe the provisions of PHIC Circular No. 10 series of 2000 on the disposition of the PHIC Capitation Fund and stop the practice of paying incentives to non-health professionals and/or volunteers.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the propriety on the disbursement of funds charged against the PHIC Capitation Fund.
observations.managementAction: The MHO was instructed to stop the payment of incentives to the non-health personnel. The expenditures for the Halad ng Barobo activity be charged to the appropriation of the MHO under the General Fund.
observations.reason: The LGU stopped the payment of incentives to the non-health personnel. Likewise, expenditures for Halad ng Barobo activity were charged against the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the documentary requirement supporting the payments for purchases of lots. Submit the all the required documents enumerated in Section 13.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured to forward the titles of the lot.
observations.reason: Some of the documents were not yet forwarded to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere to the guidelines in payment of BAC Honoraria under DBM Circular 2004-5A. Direct the Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents for further verification and to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: No comment received.
observations.reason: The DV for claims of BAC Honorarium were still incompletely supported with the necessary SDs.

observations.recommendation:
Strictly observe the replacement procedure and to direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer in coordination with the Office of the Senior Citizens Affairs to submit the required documents for social pension beneficiaries who are deceased, transferred to other location and receiving pension from private institutions or financial assistance from family members or relatives.

Submit also the approved Certificate of Eligibility from the DSWD for these new beneficiaries and justification for the replacement.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO assured to provide the required documents for verification.
observations.reason: Documents duly approved by the DSWD Regional Office No. XIII were submitted by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to submit for audit all the DVs and the necessary supporting documents for the payment of honoraria and to strictly comply the guidelines in the grant of the same.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant assured to submit the accounts to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The DVs and SDs are forwarded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly observed the prescribed rules and regulations and the rate of allowance for official travels under EO 298.  Also direct the Accountant not to process any claims without the complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: No comment received
observations.reason: The DVs were supported with the required SDs however, the rate given did not compliance to rules and regulations provided in EO No. 298.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to require the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) as basic document supporting fund transfers pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No.2012-001.

Record all fund transfer as Receivables and require the Leagues to submit liquidation report to monitor the utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Management assured to comply with the audit recommendation
observations.reason: The concerned offices executed a MOA; and the fund transfer was recorded by the Accountant under the Receivables account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the concerned Heads of Offices strictly adhere to the provisions in the use of appropriated funds in pursuant to Section 336 of the RA 7160 and Article 454 (b) (1) of its IRR. We also recommend that the Municipal Budget Officer should ensure that an appropriation ordinance should be enacted before any charges to augmented funds will be done.
observations.managementAction: No comments received.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management stop granting cash advances to elected officials other than for travelling expenses in pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002. Also, adjust the erroneous entry in recording cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Management ordered the concerned personnel to immediately liquidate their cash advances.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, previous cash advances were not yet fully liquidated. The Accountant withholds a certain amount from the payslip as liquidation of the long overdue cash advances.
